trans-unit.source: Exercise 5: Quick Map Creation - Aina Uses Map Templates
trans-unit.target: Exercise 5: Quick Map Creation - Aina Uses Map Templates

trans-unit.source: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.
trans-unit.target: This exercise can be used in online and presence training.

trans-unit.source: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.
trans-unit.target: It can be done as a follow-along exercise or individually as a self-study.

trans-unit.source: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html)
trans-unit.target: This exercise is part of the [Madagascar Anticipatory Action Cyclon Analysis Exercise Track](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Exercise_tracks/en_mdg_aa_cyclones.html)

trans-unit.source: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall.
trans-unit.target: :::{card} :class-card: sd-border-1 sd-shadow-none __Aim of the exercise:__ ^^^ Aina, the GIS expert at the Malagasy Red Cross (CRM), is preparing for the upcoming cyclone season. She wants to improve her team’s ability to act quickly once a storm is forecasted by automating key analyses in QGIS. These include estimating exposed populations, identifying impacted services like health and education, and assessing whether health posts can be reached from key warehouses within a critical 10-hour window. The goal is to prepare an end-to-end analysis and visualization workflow that can support fast, data-driven anticipatory action before a cyclone makes landfall.

trans-unit.source: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.
trans-unit.target: Take the time to familiarise yourself with the exercise and the provided material.

trans-unit.source: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.
trans-unit.target: Prepare a white-board. It can be either a physical whiteboard, a flip-chart, or a digital whiteboard (e.g. Miro board) where the participants can add their findings and questions.

trans-unit.source: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.
trans-unit.target: Before starting the exercise, make sure everybody has installed QGIS and has downloaded __and unzipped__ the data folder.

trans-unit.source: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction
trans-unit.target: Check out [How to do trainings?](https://giscience.github.io/gis-training-resource-center/content/Trainers_corner/en_how_to_training.html#how-to-do-trainings) for some general tips on training conduction

trans-unit.source: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Introduce the idea and aim of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.
trans-unit.target: Provide the download link and make sure everybody has unzipped the folder before beginning the tasks.

trans-unit.source: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Show and explain each step yourself at least twice and slow enough so everybody can see what you are doing, and follow along in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.
trans-unit.target: Make sure that everybody is following along and doing the steps themselves by periodically asking if anybody needs help or if everybody is still following.

trans-unit.source: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.
trans-unit.target: Be open and patient to every question or problem that might come up. Your participants are essentially multitasking by paying attention to your instructions and orienting themselves in their own QGIS-project.

trans-unit.source: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.
trans-unit.target: Leave time for any issues or questions concerning the tasks at the end of the exercise.

trans-unit.source: Leave some time for open questions.
trans-unit.target: Leave some time for open questions.

trans-unit.source: __Download all datasets here, save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__ :::
trans-unit.target: __Download all datasets here, save the folder on your computer and unzip the file.__ :::

trans-unit.source: After all the hard work of analyzing data and styling layers, Aina is ready to **share her results**. But creating a professional-looking map from scratch every time would be slow and repetitive.
trans-unit.target: After all the hard work of analyzing data and styling layers, Aina is ready to **share her results**. But creating a professional-looking map from scratch every time would be slow and repetitive.

trans-unit.source: To save time, she uses **map templates (.qpt files)** prepared by her team. These templates already contain the essential elements — map frames, legends, logos, titles, and scale bars. With them, Aina can turn her analysis into a **clean, consistent map** in just a few clicks.
trans-unit.target: To save time, she uses **map templates (.qpt files)** prepared by her team. These templates already contain the essential elements — map frames, legends, logos, titles, and scale bars. With them, Aina can turn her analysis into a **clean, consistent map** in just a few clicks.

trans-unit.source: ✅ **Goal** Apply a ready-made QGIS map template to quickly create and export maps that show cyclone impacts on population, health facilities, and schools.
trans-unit.target: ✅ **Goal** Apply a ready-made QGIS map template to quickly create and export maps that show cyclone impacts on population, health facilities, and schools.

trans-unit.source: Load the pre-made print layout template
trans-unit.target: Load the pre-made print layout template

trans-unit.source: Locate the template `cyclone_impact_population_map_template.qpt` in your project folder under: `Map_Templates/`
trans-unit.target: Locate the template `cyclone_impact_population_map_template.qpt` in your project folder under: `Map_Templates/`

trans-unit.source: You can load the template **by drag-and-drop**:
trans-unit.target: You can load the template **by drag-and-drop**:

trans-unit.source: Drag the `.qpt` file directly into QGIS — a new layout will be created automatically.
trans-unit.target: Drag the `.qpt` file directly into QGIS — a new layout will be created automatically.

trans-unit.source: In the layout, go to `Layout` → `Import from Template…`
trans-unit.target: In the layout, go to `Layout` → `Import from Template…`

trans-unit.source: Right-click anywhere on the white canvas and choose `Page Properties`.
trans-unit.target: Right-click anywhere on the white canvas and choose `Page Properties`.

trans-unit.source: On the right-side panel, ensure the following:
trans-unit.target: On the right-side panel, ensure the following:

trans-unit.source: Update the attribute table of exposed districts
trans-unit.target: Update the attribute table of exposed districts

trans-unit.source: In the **Print Layout**, click on the attribute table (right-hand side of the layout).
trans-unit.target: In the **Print Layout**, click on the attribute table (right-hand side of the layout).

trans-unit.source: Ensure the correct layer is selected `Harald_Exposed_population`
trans-unit.target: Ensure the correct layer is selected `Harald_Exposed_population`

trans-unit.source: Click `Attributes…` → in the upper part under **Fields** click on `Clear`
trans-unit.target: Click `Attributes…` → in the upper part under **Fields** click on `Clear`

trans-unit.source: Then add the following layer by clicking on ➕ :
trans-unit.target: Then add the following layer by clicking on ➕ :

trans-unit.source: To sort the tabel content, under the **Sorting** clicking on ➕ and add the column `AMD1_EN`
trans-unit.target: To sort the tabel content, under the **Sorting** clicking on ➕ and add the column `AMD1_EN`

trans-unit.source: :::{admonition} ⚠️ Warning – Long Tables If the attribute table you want to include is **longer than the map frame**, part of it will be cut off in the exported map. To fix this, open the table properties in the layout and **reduce the font size** until the full table fits. :::
trans-unit.target: :::{admonition} ⚠️ Warning – Long Tables If the attribute table you want to include is **longer than the map frame**, part of it will be cut off in the exported map. To fix this, open the table properties in the layout and **reduce the font size** until the full table fits. :::

trans-unit.source: In the layout, click on the **Legend** item.
trans-unit.target: In the layout, click on the **Legend** item.

trans-unit.source: Scroll to **Legend items** and remove all entries (🗑️)
trans-unit.target: Scroll to **Legend items** and remove all entries (🗑️)

trans-unit.source: When selecting layers, check **Only visible layers**
trans-unit.target: When selecting layers, check **Only visible layers**

trans-unit.source: Rename legend entries to match layout naming
trans-unit.target: Rename legend entries to match layout naming

trans-unit.source: The logos that need to be added to the map are represented by the red **X**.
trans-unit.target: The logos that need to be added to the map are represented by the red **X**.

trans-unit.source: Click on the image in the **Item List**.
trans-unit.target: Click on the image in the **Item List**.

trans-unit.source: Click on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to the file path.
trans-unit.target: Click on the three dots ![](/fig/Three_points.png) next to the file path.

trans-unit.source: Browse to the folder `logos_pictures` and select the correct logo file.
trans-unit.target: Browse to the folder `logos_pictures` and select the correct logo file.

trans-unit.source: Review and update layout text elements
trans-unit.target: Review and update layout text elements

trans-unit.source: Make sure all text elements are up to date, especially:
trans-unit.target: Make sure all text elements are up to date, especially:

trans-unit.source: Adjust font size or alignment if necessary
trans-unit.target: Adjust font size or alignment if necessary

trans-unit.source: ::::{dropdown} Your final output should look like this after styling the layer The map now clearly displays the exposed population within the affected districts The original storm track line — used as input data — is highlighted, as well as the buffered impact area, which serves as a proxy for identifying exposed districts.
trans-unit.target: ::::{dropdown} Your final output should look like this after styling the layer The map now clearly displays the exposed population within the affected districts The original storm track line — used as input data — is highlighted, as well as the buffered impact area, which serves as a proxy for identifying exposed districts.

trans-unit.source: On the right-hand side of the map, a list shows all exposed districts, including data on total population and exposed population. The districts (Admin 2) are organized under their corresponding regions (Admin 1).
trans-unit.target: On the right-hand side of the map, a list shows all exposed districts, including data on total population and exposed population. The districts (Admin 2) are organized under their corresponding regions (Admin 1).
